title: Pending Telegram Callbacks
description: Append-only stash of context for Telegram inline-button callbacks. Written by the Kaggle Email Watcher workflow when a notification is sent (one entry per outbound notification, indexed by shortid). Read by the Telegram Callback Handler when the user clicks a button (lookup by shortid encoded in callback_data) so downstream consumers (#46 skip, #28 accept) get a full context object — slug, URL, name, Gmail email_id, Telegram chat_id and message_id — without re-deriving anything. Entries older than 30 days are pruned by the writer on every append to keep the file bounded.

shortid.description: Deterministic short hash used as the callback_data suffix (j:<shortid> / s:<shortid>). Generated by the writer as crypto.createHash('sha256').update(competition_id + ts).digest('hex').slice(0, 8). Stays well under Telegram's 64-byte callback_data cap regardless of slug length.

competition_id.description: Kaggle competition slug used in the URL (e.g. 'titanic'). Same value as the watchlist's competition_id field for cross-reference.

competition_url.description: Canonical kaggle.com URL of the competition, captured at email-arrival time.

competition_name.description: Human-readable name parsed from the Launch email subject.

chat_id.description: Telegram chat id where the original notification was sent. Stored to let downstream branches editMessageText with the right scope.

message_id.description: Telegram message id of the original notification. May be null at write time (the message has not been sent yet when the stash is written) and is otherwise authoritative from the live callback_query payload at click time. Kept in the schema for future round-trip persistence patterns.

email_id.description: Gmail message id captured from the Gmail Trigger output. Required so the #46 skip branch can mark the source email as read + label it via the Gmail API without re-querying by subject.

ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the stash entry was written. Used by the writer's 30-day pruning rule and by the deterministic shortid hash input.
